title: AdbServer: any command with stdout larger than the OS pipe buffer always fails with TIMEOUT — CmdCommandPerformer reads process output only after waitFor(), causing a deadlock
author: ZuevKirill95
body:
**Describe the bug**

`CmdCommandPerformer` in `adbserver-desktop` starts a process and reads its stdout only
**after** waiting for its completion:

```kotlin
// adb-server/adbserver-desktop/src/main/java/com/kaspersky/adbserver/desktop/CmdCommandPerformer.kt
val process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec(command)
...
if (process.waitFor(executionTimeoutSeconds, TimeUnit.SECONDS)) {
    ...
    process.inputStream.bufferedReader().readText() // <- stdout is read only AFTER waitFor
```

If the command prints more than the OS pipe buffer can hold (~64 KB on Linux), the child
process blocks on writing to stdout, `waitFor()` never completes, and after 120 s the test
fails with `AdbServerException` (`CommandResult(status=TIMEOUT)`) — even though the command
itself finishes instantly in a shell. This is the classic `Runtime.exec` deadlock described
in the `java.lang.Process` javadoc.

The process is not slow — it is blocked forever, so raising the timeout (#745 / ISSUE-743)
does not help.

We hit this on a trivial `ls -A1 <dir>` over a directory with ~1500 files:

```
com.kaspersky.kaspresso.internal.exceptions.AdbServerException:
AdbServer. The command=ls -A1 ... was performed with timeout exception.
```

Desktop log at the moment of failure (TIMEOUT exactly 120 s after the command was received;
commands before and after succeed, so the jar and the connection are healthy):

```
17:22:44.951  The received command to execute: CmdCommand(body=ls -A1 ..., arguments=[])
17:24:44.953  The result: CommandResult(status=TIMEOUT, description=Command execution timeout (120 sec) overhead, ...)
```

This is most likely also the actual root cause of #582: there `pm list packages` (large
output) timed out while `pm list packages <package>` (small output) worked — the filter
reduced the output size, not the execution time.

**To Reproduce**

Pure JDK demo (any machine, Java 9+) — the exact pattern used by `CmdCommandPerformer`:

```bash
jshell - <<'EOF'
var p = Runtime.getRuntime().exec("seq 1 30000");   // ~170 KB of stdout
System.out.println("finished in 5s: " + p.waitFor(5, java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit.SECONDS));
p.destroy();
EOF
```

Prints `finished in 5s: false` — the process hangs. With `seq 1 100` it prints `true`.

Same at the unit level (no device or emulator needed):

```kotlin
val performer = CmdCommandPerformer("test-desktop", logger, executionTimeoutSeconds = 5)
val result = performer.perform("seq 1 30000")
// Actual: CommandResult(status=TIMEOUT, "Command execution timeout (5 sec) overhead")
// Expected: SUCCESS with the full output
```

**Expected behavior**

Commands are only limited by the execution timeout, not by the size of their output.
stdout/stderr should be consumed concurrently with `waitFor()` (background reader threads),
or redirected to temp files via `ProcessBuilder.redirectOutput/redirectError`.

**Additional context**

Reproduced on Kaspresso 1.5.4–1.6.1; the affected code is unchanged in master.

title: Publish adbserver-desktop.jar to Maven repo
author: victorsmirnov
body:
Suggestion: **publish adbserver-desktop.jar in Maven** repository with name "com.kaspersky.android-components:adb-server-desktop".

How does it help?

1. Use Gradle dependency management to download and cache `adbserver-desktop.jar` instead of custom scripts or manual updates.
2. Use corporate Maven proxy in CI the same way as for other Kaspresso libraries, no need for any custom logic to get JAR file on corporate CI servers.

Example for Gradle Kotlin DSL

```
val adbServerConfig by configurations.creating
dependencies {
    adbServerConfig("com.kaspersky.android-components:adb-server-desktop:1.6.0")
}
```

Now I publish `adbserver-desktop.jar` in our company Maven repo. Having it in the central Kaspresso repo would simplify our CI.

Thank you for your work and support!

title: Add configurable screenshotMaker to Kaspresso.Builder
author: Twinsen81
body:
## Problem

`InternalScreenshotMaker` uses `Activity.window.decorView.draw(Canvas)` which only captures the main activity window. Views rendered in separate popup windows — such as Material 3's `ModalBottomSheet` — are invisible in the resulting screenshot.

`CombinedScreenshotMaker` falls back to `ExternalScreenshotMaker` (which uses `UiDevice.takeScreenshot()` and captures the full screen) only when the internal one throws an exception:

```kotlin
runCatching {
    preferredScreenshotMaker.takeScreenshot(file)
}.onFailure {
    fallbackScreenshotMaker.takeScreenshot(file)
}
```

But `InternalScreenshotMaker` doesn't throw — it succeeds and produces an incomplete image. So the fallback never runs.

## Current workaround

There is no clean way to swap the screenshot maker. The `screenshotMaker` is constructed inline inside `postInitVariables()` and not exposed as a configurable property.

The only working workaround is to avoid `advanced()` entirely, use `simple()`, manually reconstruct `ScreenshotsImpl` with all its dependencies in `.apply`, and re-add interceptors by hand:

```kotlin
val E2ETestsBuilder = Kaspresso.Builder.simple {
    flakySafetyParams = FlakySafetyParams.custom(timeoutMs = 1_000)
}.apply {
    screenshots = ScreenshotsImpl(
        logger = libLogger,
        resourceFilesProvider = resourceFilesProvider,
        screenshotMaker = ExternalScreenshotMaker(
            instrumentalDependencyProviderFactory.getComponentProvider<ExternalScreenshotMaker>(
                instrumentation
            ),
            screenshotParams
        ),
        visualTestParams = visualTestParams,
        screenshotsComparator = screenshotsComparator,
        dirsProvider = dirsProvider,
        resourceFileNamesProvider = resourceFileNamesProvider,
        resourcesDirsProvider = resourcesDirsProvider,
    )
    stepWatcherInterceptors.add(ScreenshotStepWatcherInterceptor(screenshots))
    testRunWatcherInterceptors.add(VideoRecordingInterceptor(videos))
    stepParams.autonumber = true
}
```

This is fragile — it depends on Builder internals, and using `advanced()` or `withForcedAllureSupport()` instead of `simple()` doesn't work because those builders wire screenshot interceptors before `.apply` runs, capturing a stale reference to the old `screenshots` object.

## Proposed fix

1. Add a `lateinit var screenshotMaker: ScreenshotMaker` property to `Kaspresso.Builder`, following the same `if (!::prop.isInitialized)` pattern used by other Builder properties. The default behavior is unchanged — if not set, the existing `CombinedScreenshotMaker` is created.

2. Add a secondary constructor to `ExternalScreenshotMaker` that accepts `Instrumentation` directly, so users don't need to deal with `InstrumentalDependencyProvider`.

3. Fix `ExternalScreenshotMaker.takeFullWindowScreenshot` which currently throws `TODO("not yet implemented")`. Since `UiDevice.takeScreenshot()` already captures the full screen, it can delegate to `takeScreenshot()`.

This reduces the workaround above to a single line that works with any builder — `simple()`, `advanced()`, or `withForcedAllureSupport()`:

```kotlin
Kaspresso.Builder.advanced {
    screenshotMaker = ExternalScreenshotMaker(instrumentation)
}
```

title: Default flakiness behaviour stops after exception even though it's caught
author: vvasilev-wyn
body:
**Describe the bug**
I have some logic to click an indeterminate number of times until the element is no longer visible:
```
        for (i in 0..50) {
            val result = runCatching {
                flakySafely(200) {
                    SubstitutionPromptScreen.selectDifferentSubstitute.click()
                }
            }
            if (result.isFailure) break
        }
```
This works as expected. However after that it seems the default flakiness behavior stops working correctly - the test fails with NoMatchingViewException on a view that appears definitely in time - and there is no retrying with the default timeout of 10 sec. If I manually add the flakySafely block where the test fails, it passes. If I make sure there is no exception - like repeating the click the exact number of times, after the test passes as well.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Make sure an exception is thrown from a Kakao element and is caught
2. Perform a view assertion that will need to retry.
3. Test fails with NoMatchingViewException

**Expected behavior**
Catching the exception should not remove the default flakiness behavior.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: [e.g. iOS]
 - Browser [e.g. chrome, safari]
 - Version [e.g. 22]

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: [e.g. iPhone6]
 - OS: [e.g. iOS8.1]
 - Browser [e.g. stock browser, safari]
 - Version [e.g. 22]

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Images are not being uploaded to the Allure report
author: mathpi314159-fap
body:
**Describe the bug**
The report generated by Allure does not contain images for failed tests.
I use a custom builder in the tests:
```
val kaspressoBuilder = Kaspresso.Builder.withForcedAllureSupport().addComposeSupport(
    lateComposeCustomize = { composeBuilder ->
        composeBuilder.semanticsBehaviorInterceptors =
            composeBuilder.semanticsBehaviorInterceptors.filter {
                it !is SystemDialogSafetySemanticsBehaviorInterceptor
            }.toMutableList()
        flakySafetyParams = FlakySafetyParams.custom(timeoutMs = 10_000, intervalMs = 100)
    }
).apply {
    testRunWatcherInterceptors.addAll((listOf(DumpViewsInterceptor(viewHierarchyDumper))))
    screenshotParams = ScreenshotParams(quality = 0)
}
```
I have granted all the necessary authorizations:
```
class LoginTest : TestCase(kaspressoBuilder) {

    @get:Rule
    val grantPermissionRule: GrantPermissionRule = GrantPermissionRule.grant(
        android.Manifest.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS,
        android.Manifest.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE,
        android.Manifest.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE
    )

    @get:Rule
    val composeTestRule = createAndroidComposeRule(MainActivity::class.java)
...
```
build.gradle.kts
```
defaultConfig {
  testInstrumentationRunner = "com.kaspersky.kaspresso.runner.KaspressoRunner"
  testInstrumentationRunnerArguments["clearPackageData"] = "true"
  testInstrumentationRunnerArguments["useTestStorageService"] = "true"
}
```

title: Permissions.denyViaDialog() only works once
author: JordanLongstaff
body:
**Describe the bug**
A user can click the **Deny** button in the permissions dialog to dismiss it without granting the permission. But as of Android 11, if the app asks about the permission again, the **Deny** button will have a different ID, one that Kaspresso currently doesn't know about, so trying to press it again fails.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. The app should ask for a runtime permission.
2. Call `denyViaDialog()`.
3. The app should ask for the same permission again.
4. Try to call `denyViaDialog()` again.

**Expected behavior**
The **Deny** button gets clicked both times.

**Actual behavior**
The second click of the **Deny** button never happens.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6e40e90-a3b3-4d7b-a488-cebd84de3fce

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Google Pixel 7
 - OS: Android 15

**Additional context**
#667 added an option to specify a different **Allow** button than the default. This issue can easily be fixed by adding a button with the resource ID `permission_deny_and_dont_ask_again_button` and an optional parameter to `denyViaDialog()`.

title: Kotest support
author: JordanLongstaff
body:
[Kotest](https://kotest.io) is a testing framework designed for Kotlin. It's an alternative to Java that I really love using. It's more flexible and has coroutine support, and there's also an [Android runner for it](https://github.com/LeoColman/kotest-android).

Right now I'm trying to switch all my instrumented tests over to Kaspresso. It'd be easier to do if I could integrate it with my previous efforts to switch from JUnit to Kotest, but I can't find a way to combine Kotest specs with Kaspresso provided objects, like Device.

title: android.enableAppCompileTimeRClass=true causes UiViewBuilder#withId(int) to crash
author: Mexator
body:
**Describe the bug**

The exception is
```
java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: com.example.ui.test.R$id
 at java.lang.Class.classForName(Native Method)
 at java.lang.Class.forName(Class.java:454)
 at java.lang.Class.forName(Class.java:379)
 at com.kaspersky.components.kautomator.common.resources.RClassProvider.provideClass(RClassProvider.kt:10)
 at com.kaspersky.components.kautomator.common.resources.ResourceNameProvider.resolveResName(ResourceNameProvider.kt:7)
 at com.kaspersky.components.kautomator.component.common.builders.UiViewBuilder.withId(UiViewBuilder.kt:51)
...
```

The code that causes exception to appear is simple interaction with any `UiView` using an R class:

```kotlin
UiScrollView {
    withId(R.id.accountsListRecyclerView) // crashes
    withResourceName("accountsListRecyclerView") // works fine
}.scrollToEnd()
```

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Add `android.enableAppCompileTimeRClass=true` into your `gradle.properties` 
2. Run any KAutomator test that uses UiView declared as above

**Expected behavior**
Test runs fine

**Additional context**
The issue is that when this flag is set, the application R class does not exist in runtime. Instead, all constants are inlined.

The current implementation of `com.kaspersky.components.kautomator.common.resources.ResourceNameProvider` relies on reflection and parsing of R class fields. One possible solution (which we incorporated into client code) is to get resource name from target context resources. This goes as follows:
```kotlin
private fun getResourceName(id: Int): String {
    return InstrumentationRegistry.getInstrumentation().targetContext.resources.getResourceName(id)
}
```

author: cobaltvo

title: Optional `expect` block as a part of framework
author: phansier
body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** We have discovered in support chat, that some users do their own blocks instead of `step` for making assertions after step. I suppose it could be helpful, if such option would be part of the framework and documentation on test structure. Adding this alternative to step can be also helpful for synchronisation of test code with TMS, like: - `step` - contains description of Action field - `expect` - contains description of Checks/Expected field The main goal of this issue is to start discussion of implementation variants. **Describe the solution you'd like** Current solution we have in my project is: ``` fun TestContext<*>.expect(description: String, actions: (StepInfo) -> Unit) = this.step(description, actions) ``` This does not put any restrictions or obligations on test structure though. Expect block can be either at the same level as step, or inside of step. **Describe alternatives you've considered** №1: Use nested steps: ``` step { // will be enumerated like 1 // actions step { // will be enumerated like 1.1 // asserions } } ``` (to be continued) **Additional context**

title: Views metadata is not recorded for compose screens
author: Nikitae57
body: Doc loc tests do not record metadata xml files for compose screens
